summary: Iteration 2 testing of Tacos POS multi-user/multi-sucursal release. Backend: 18 pytest cases (sucursales, login admin+5 cashiers, sales validation 422/400, sales+dashboard with sucursal/tip_breakdown filters, users list+update password+sucursal) all PASS. Frontend: Login redirects (admin->/admin, cashier->/pos), route guards work (cashier blocked from /admin), POS shows correct sucursal in header, BEBIDA/COMIDA tags with sky/emerald, long names render full, compact cart panel shows Subtotal+Propina line only when tip>0, 2 Tacos + tarjeta + tip 25 = $85 confirmed, charge clears cart and persists. Admin: all 5 sucursal buttons, KPIs, tip-breakdown (tarjeta/transferencia/total), Mezcalitos filter shows the $30 tarjeta tip from the created sale, sales table has all required columns (Hora/Sucursal/Cajero/Productos/Subtotal/Propina/Total/Pago), 6 user rows, password edit via UI immediately allows new password login via API, shadcn calendar popover opens.
critical_code_review_comments: POS.jsx admin fallback: when an admin logs in and somehow lands on /pos, sucursal defaults to 'Valle Dorado'. Reasonable for testing but consider showing a sucursal switcher for admins instead of a silent default.
auth.js stores the session (incl. token) in plain localStorage; acceptable for an internal POS but flag for any future public-facing deployment.
server.py /api/auth/login stores and compares passwords in PLAINTEXT in MongoDB. For an internal MVP this matches the request, but a hashing step (bcrypt) should be on the roadmap.
server.py /api/users PUT has NO auth gating — anyone reaching the URL can change any user's password. Add an admin token check before exposing publicly.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan.

POS.jsx: cart-subtotal element is rendered twice (visible variant when tip>0 and hidden span when tip===0) sharing the same data-testid. Two elements with the same testid can confuse test runners; consider rendering only one.
Dashboard polling fires every 5s with 4 endpoints (dashboard, sales, products, users) without pausing on tab hidden — fine for MVP but worth a visibility check.
test_credentials: admin/taco123, valle_dorado/valle123, mezcalitos/mezca123, san_vicente/vicente123, pi/pi123, san_jose/jose123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md + DEFAULT_USERS in server.py)
seed_data_creation: Tests created ~1 tarjeta sale (Mezcalitos, $90 total, $30 tip) plus the POS UI test created 1 sale (Valle Dorado, $85, $25 tip). Password updates were performed and restored to original values.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the source of truth for backend regression (18 cases, parametrized for all 5 cashiers). Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://mobile-checkout-pos.preview.emergentagent.com pytest backend/tests/. Frontend tested on mobile viewport 390x844 for POS and desktop 1280x900 for Admin. Note that /api/sucursales GET happens on Admin mount; the suc-{Name} buttons render only after that resolves — wait 1-2s before asserting. The cart-subtotal data-testid is duplicated (visible + hidden) in POS.jsx; prefer asserting text via text_content. Sales DB now contains records for both Mezcalitos and Valle Dorado for today — re-running tests will keep accumulating sales.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs found in this iteration.
